questions.question: A trapezium has an area of $105\text{ cm}^2$ and parallel sides of length $5\text{ cm}$ and $9\text{ cm}$. Find the perpendicular distance between the parallel sides.
questions.explanation:
$105 = 0.5 \times (5 + 9) \times h \Rightarrow 105 = 7h \Rightarrow h = 15\text{ cm}$.

**Common mistake:** A student might forget to divide the sum of parallel sides by 2 before solving for $h.

questions.question: A rectangular field is $400\text{ m}$ long and has an area of^6 hectares. Calculate the perimeter of the field ($1\text{ hectare} = 10000\text{ m}^2$).
questions.explanation:
Area $= 60000\text{ m}^2$. Width $= 60000 \div 400 = 150\text{ m}$. Perimeter $= 2(400 + 150) = 1100\text{ m}$.

**Common mistake:** A common mistake is finding the width and assuming it is the final answer for the perimeter.

questions.question: Find the radius of a circle that has a circumference of $20\text{ m}$. Give your answer to 3 significant figures.
questions.explanation:
$C = 2\pi r \Rightarrow 20 = 2\pi r \Rightarrow r = 10 \div \pi \approx 3.183$..., so $3.18\text{ m}$ (3 s.f.).

**Common mistake:** The student might divide by $\pi^2 6.37$.

questions.question: A circle of radius $5\text{ cm}$ is inscribed inside a square. Calculate the shaded area remaining in the square after the circle is removed.
questions.explanation:
Side of square $= 2 \times \text{radius} = 10\text{ cm}$. Square area $= 100$. Circle area $= \pi \times 5^2 \approx 78.54$. Shaded area $= 100 - 78.54 = 21.46 \approx 21.5$.

**Common mistake:** The student might think the side of the square is equal to the radius instead of the diameter.

questions.question: Calculate the area of $\Delta ABC$. So $a = 7\text{ cm}$, side $b = 14\text{ cm}$, and angle $C = 80^{\circ}$.
questions.explanation:
Area $= 0.5 \times a \times b \times \sin C = 0.5 \times 7 \times 14 \times \sin 80^{\circ} \approx 48.256$.

**Common mistake:** The student might forget the $0.5$ in the formula or assume it is a right-angled triangle.
